operations\n- [ ] Unit tests written for new services\n- [ ] Integration tests passing in staging\n\n### Database Backup\n\n```bash\n# Full database backup before migration\npg_dump -U vivim_user -h localhost vivim_db \\\n  --format=custom \\\n  --compress=9 \\\n  --file=vivim_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump\n\n# Verify backup\npg_restore --list vivim_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump\n\n# Store backup in secure location\naws s3 cp vivim_backup_*.dump s3://vivim-backups/pre-migration/\n```\n\n---\n\n## Phase 1: Corpus Core Models Migration\n\n**Migration Name:** `add_corpus_core_models`  \n**Duration:** 2-4 hours (depends on data volume)  \n**Risk:** Low (all new models, no existing changes)\n\n### 1.1 Schema Changes\n\n```prisma\n// NEW MODELS (no existing models modified)\nmodel Tenant { }\nmodel CorpusDocument { }\nmodel CorpusDocumentVersion { }\nmodel CorpusChunk { }\nmodel CorpusTopic { }\nmodel CorpusFAQ { }\n\n// NEW ENUMS\nenum UserAvatar { STRANGER, ACQUAINTANCE, FAMILIAR, KNOWN }\nenum TopicScope { USER, CORPUS }\nenum ChunkContentType { prose, code, table, list, mixed }\nenum DocumentChangeType { major, minor, patch }\nenum OrchestrationIntent { ... }\nenum ConversationSentiment { positive, neutral, negative, mixed }\nenum ResolutionStatus { resolved, pending, escalated, unknown }\n```\n\n### 1.2 Migration Command\n\n```bash\n# Step 1: Create migration\nnpx prisma migrate dev --name add_corpus_core_models\n\n# Step 2: Review generated SQL\ncat prisma/migrations/*/migration.sql\n\n# Step 3: Apply to production\nnpx prisma migrate deploy\n```\n\n### 1.3 Generated SQL (Preview)\n\n```sql\n-- Create enums\nCREATE TYPE \"UserAvatar\" AS ENUM ('STRANGER', 'ACQUAINTANCE', 'FAMILIAR', 'KNOWN');\nCREATE TYPE \"TopicScope\" AS ENUM ('USER', 'CORPUS');\nCREATE TYPE \"ChunkContentType\" AS ENUM ('prose', 'code', 'table', 'list', 'mixed');\nCREATE TYPE \"DocumentChangeType\" AS ENUM ('major', 'minor', 'patch');\nCREATE TYPE \"OrchestrationIntent\" AS ENUM (...);\nCREATE TYPE \"ConversationSentiment\" AS ENUM ('positive', 'neutral', 'negative', 'mixed');\nCREATE TYPE \"ResolutionStatus\" AS ENUM ('resolved', 'pending', 'escalated', 'unknown');\n\n-- Create tables\nCREATE TABLE \"tenants\" (\n  \"id\" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n  \"name\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n  \"slug\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n  \"chatbotConfig\" JSONB,\n  \"brandVoice\" JSONB,\n  \"guardrails\" TEXT[],\n  \"defaultModel\" TEXT DEFAULT 'gpt-4o',\n  \"embeddingModel\" TEXT DEFAULT 'text-embedding-3-small',\n  \"maxContextTokens\" INTEGER DEFAULT 12000,\n  \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  \"updatedAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  PRIMARY KEY (\"id\")\n);\n\nCREATE TABLE \"corpus_documents\" ( ... );\nCREATE TABLE \"corpus_document_versions\" ( ... );\nCREATE TABLE \"corpus_chunks\" ( ... );\nCREATE TABLE \"corpus_topics\" ( ... );\nCREATE TABLE \"corpus_faqs\" ( ... );\n\n-- Create indexes\nCREATE INDEX \"tenants_slug_idx\" ON \"tenants\"(\"slug\");\nCREATE INDEX \"corpus_chunks_tenantId_embedding_idx\" ON \"corpus_chunks\"(\"tenantId\", \"embedding\");\n-- ... more indexes\n\n-- Enable pgvector (if not already enabled)\nCREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;\n```\n\n### 1.4 Post-Migration Verification\n\n```bash\n# Verify tables created\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\dt tenants corpus_*\"\n\n# Verify enums created\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\dT UserAvatar TopicScope\"\n\n# Verify indexes created\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\di tenants_* corpus_*\"\n\n# Run health check\ncurl http://localhost:3000/api/health\n```\n\n### 1.5 Rollback Plan\n\n```bash\n# Rollback migration\nnpx prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back add_corpus_core_models\n\n# Or manually drop tables\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"corpus_faqs\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"corpus_chunks\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"corpus_topics\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"corpus_document_versions\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"corpus_documents\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"tenants\" CASCADE;\nDROP TYPE IF EXISTS \"UserAvatar\";\nDROP TYPE IF EXISTS \"TopicScope\";\n-- ... drop other enums\nEOF\n```\n\n### 1.6 Success Criteria\n\n- [ ] All tables created successfully\n- [ ] All enums created successfully\n- [ ] All indexes created successfully\n- [ ] pgvector extension enabled\n- [ ] No errors in migration logs\n- [ ] Health check passes\n- [ ] Existing queries unaffected\n\n---\n\n## Phase 2: VirtualUser Extensions Migration\n\n**Migration Name:** `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields`  \n**Duration:** 1-2 hours  \n**Risk:** Low (nullable fields, backward compatible)\n\n### 2.1 Schema Changes\n\n```prisma\nmodel VirtualUser {\n  // ... EXISTING FIELDS ...\n\n  // NEW: Tenant association (nullable, backward compatible)\n  tenantId              String?\n  tenant                Tenant? @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)\n\n  // NEW: Avatar classification (with default)\n  currentAvatar         String    @default(\"STRANGER\")\n  profileVersion        Int       @default(0)\n\n  // NEW: Relations (additive, no breaking changes)\n  conversationIndices   ConversationIndex[]\n  profileSnapshots      UserProfileSnapshot[]\n}\n```\n\n### 2.2 Migration Command\n\n```bash\n# Create migration\nnpx prisma migrate dev --name add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields\n\n# Apply to production\nnpx prisma migrate deploy\n```\n\n### 2.3 Generated SQL (Preview)\n\n```sql\n-- Add columns to existing VirtualUser table\nALTER TABLE \"virtual_users\"\n  ADD COLUMN \"tenantId\" UUID,\n  ADD COLUMN \"currentAvatar\" \"UserAvatar\" NOT NULL DEFAULT 'STRANGER',\n  ADD COLUMN \"profileVersion\" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;\n\n-- Add foreign key constraint\nALTER TABLE \"virtual_users\"\n  ADD CONSTRAINT \"virtual_users_tenantId_fkey\"\n  FOREIGN KEY (\"tenantId\")\n  REFERENCES \"tenants\"(\"id\")\n  ON DELETE SET NULL;\n\n-- Create index for tenant lookup\nCREATE INDEX \"virtual_users_tenantId_idx\" ON \"virtual_users\"(\"tenantId\");\n\n-- Create index for avatar classification\nCREATE INDEX \"virtual_users_currentAvatar_idx\" ON \"virtual_users\"(\"currentAvatar\");\n```\n\n### 2.4 Data Migration (Optional)\n\nIf you want to pre-populate `currentAvatar` based on existing data:\n\n```sql\n-- Update avatar based on existing conversation count\nUPDATE \"virtual_users\" vu\nSET \"currentAvatar\" = CASE\n  WHEN vu.\"conversationCount\" = 0 THEN 'STRANGER'\n  WHEN vu.\"conversationCount\" <= 5 THEN 'ACQUAINTANCE'\n  WHEN vu.\"conversationCount\" <= 20 THEN 'FAMILIAR'\n  ELSE 'KNOWN'\nEND\nWHERE \"currentAvatar\" = 'STRANGER';  -- Only update default values\n```\n\n### 2.5 Post-Migration Verification\n\n```bash\n# Verify columns added\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\d virtual_users\"\n\n# Verify foreign key\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\df virtual_users_tenantId_fkey\"\n\n# Test existing VirtualUser queries still work\ncurl http://localhost:3000/api/v1/virtual/profile?virtualUserId=test-uuid\n\n# Verify new fields are nullable/defaults\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"SELECT id, tenantId, currentAvatar FROM virtual_users LIMIT 5\"\n```\n\n### 2.6 Rollback Plan\n\n```bash\n# Rollback migration\nnpx prisma migrate rollback --name add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields\n\n# Or manually revert\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF\nALTER TABLE \"virtual_users\"\n  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS \"tenantId\",\n  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS \"currentAvatar\",\n  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS \"profileVersion\";\n\nDROP INDEX IF EXISTS \"virtual_users_tenantId_idx\";\nDROP INDEX IF EXISTS \"virtual_users_currentAvatar_idx\";\nEOF\n```\n\n### 2.7 Success Criteria\n\n- [ ] Columns added successfully\n- [ ] Foreign key constraint created\n- [ ] Indexes created\n- [ ] Existing VirtualUser queries work\n- [ ] New fields have correct defaults\n- [ ] No NULL constraint violations\n\n---\n\n## Phase 3: Conversation Awareness Models\n\n**Migration Name:** `add_conversation_awareness_models`  \n**Duration:** 2-3 hours  \n**Risk:** Low (new companion models)\n\n### 3.1 Schema Changes\n\n```prisma\n// NEW MODELS\nmodel ConversationIndex {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  virtualUserId       String\n  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(...)\n  conversationId      String    @unique\n  // ... other fields\n}\n\nmodel UserProfileSnapshot {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  virtualUserId       String\n  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(...)\n  avatar              String\n  version             Int       @default(1)\n  // ... other fields\n}\n```\n\n### 3.2 Migration Command\n\n```bash\n# Create migration\nnpx prisma migrate dev --name add_conversation_awareness_models\n\n# Apply to production\nnpx prisma migrate deploy\n```\n\n### 3.3 Generated SQL (Preview)\n\n```sql\n-- Create ConversationIndex table\nCREATE TABLE \"conversation_indices\" (\n  \"id\" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n  \"virtualUserId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"conversationId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"summary\" TEXT NOT NULL,\n  \"embedding\" vector(1536),\n  \"topics\" TEXT[],\n  \"keyFacts\" JSONB,\n  \"questionsAsked\" TEXT[],\n  \"issuesDiscussed\" TEXT[],\n  \"decisionsReached\" TEXT[],\n  \"actionItems\" TEXT[],\n  \"messageCount\" INTEGER,\n  \"duration\" INTEGER,\n  \"sentiment\" \"ConversationSentiment\",\n  \"resolutionStatus\" \"ResolutionStatus\",\n  \"startedAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),\n  \"endedAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),\n  \"lastReferencedAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),\n  \"referenceCount\" INTEGER DEFAULT 0,\n  \"relatedConversationIds\" TEXT[],\n  \"memoryIds\" TEXT[],\n  \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  PRIMARY KEY (\"id\"),\n  CONSTRAINT \"conversation_indices_conversationId_unique\" UNIQUE (\"conversationId\"),\n  CONSTRAINT \"conversation_indices_virtualUserId_fkey\"\n    FOREIGN KEY (\"virtualUserId\")\n    REFERENCES \"virtual_users\"(\"id\")\n    ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Create UserProfileSnapshot table\nCREATE TABLE \"user_profile_snapshots\" (\n  \"id\" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n  \"virtualUserId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"avatar\" \"UserAvatar\" NOT NULL,\n  \"version\" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,\n  \"identity\" JSONB,\n  \"preferences\" JSONB,\n  \"topicExpertise\" JSONB,\n  \"behavior\" JSONB,\n  \"activeConcerns\" JSONB,\n  \"evolutionLog\" JSONB,\n  \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  \"lastEvolvedAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  PRIMARY KEY (\"id\"),\n  CONSTRAINT \"user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_version_unique\"\n    UNIQUE (\"virtualUserId\", \"version\"),\n  CONSTRAINT \"user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_fkey\"\n    FOREIGN KEY (\"virtualUserId\")\n    REFERENCES \"virtual_users\"(\"id\")\n    ON DELETE CASCADE\n);\n\n-- Create indexes\nCREATE INDEX \"conversation_indices_virtualUserId_startedAt_idx\" ON \"conversation_indices\"(\"virtualUserId\", \"startedAt\");\nCREATE INDEX \"conversation_indices_virtualUserId_embedding_idx\" ON \"conversation_indices\"(\"virtualUserId\", \"embedding\");\nCREATE INDEX \"conversation_indices_virtualUserId_topics_idx\" ON \"conversation_indices\"(\"virtualUserId\", \"topics\");\nCREATE INDEX \"user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_idx\" ON \"user_profile_snapshots\"(\"virtualUserId\");\n```\n\n### 3.4 Post-Migration Verification\n\n```bash\n# Verify tables created\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\d conversation_indices user_profile_snapshots\"\n\n# Verify unique constraint on conversationId\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\d conversation_indices\"\n\n# Verify vector column\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'conversation_indices' AND column_name = 'embedding'\"\n\n# Test existing Conversation queries still work\ncurl http://localhost:3000/api/v1/virtual/conversations?virtualUserId=test-uuid\n```\n\n### 3.5 Rollback Plan\n\n```bash\n# Rollback migration\nnpx prisma migrate rollback --name add_conversation_awareness_models\n\n# Or manually drop tables\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"user_profile_snapshots\" CASCADE;\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"conversation_indices\" CASCADE;\nEOF\n```\n\n### 3.6 Success Criteria\n\n- [ ] Tables created successfully\n- [ ] Foreign key constraints created\n- [ ] Unique constraint on conversationId\n- [ ] Vector column created\n- [ ] Indexes created\n- [ ] Existing Conversation queries work\n- [ ] No cascade delete issues\n\n---\n\n## Phase 4: Telemetry + Final Polish\n\n**Migration Name:** `add_orchestration_telemetry`  \n**Duration:** 1 hour  \n**Risk:** Minimal (pure telemetry, no existing model changes)\n\n### 4.1 Schema Changes\n\n```prisma\n// NEW MODEL\nmodel OrchestrationLog {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  tenantId            String\n  virtualUserId       String\n  conversationId      String\n  // ... telemetry fields\n}\n```\n\n### 4.2 Migration Command\n\n```bash\n# Create migration\nnpx prisma migrate dev --name add_orchestration_telemetry\n\n# Apply to production\nnpx prisma migrate deploy\n```\n\n### 4.3 Generated SQL (Preview)\n\n```sql\n-- Create OrchestrationLog table\nCREATE TABLE \"orchestration_logs\" (\n  \"id\" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),\n  \"tenantId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"virtualUserId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"conversationId\" UUID NOT NULL,\n  \"intent\" \"OrchestrationIntent\" NOT NULL,\n  \"intentConfidence\" DOUBLE PRECISION,\n  \"avatar\" \"UserAvatar\" NOT NULL,\n  \"corpusWeight\" DOUBLE PRECISION,\n  \"userWeight\" DOUBLE PRECISION,\n  \"assemblyTimeMs\" INTEGER,\n  \"totalTokens\" INTEGER,\n  \"corpusTokens\" INTEGER,\n  \"userTokens\" INTEGER,\n  \"historyTokens\" INTEGER,\n  \"corpusConfidence\" DOUBLE PRECISION,\n  \"chunksRetrieved\" INTEGER,\n  \"memoriesUsed\" INTEGER,\n  \"proactiveInsights\" INTEGER,\n  \"userSatisfaction\" TEXT,\n  \"followUpRequired\" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,\n  \"escalated\" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,\n  \"createdAt\" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,\n  PRIMARY KEY (\"id\")\n);\n\n-- Create indexes\nCREATE INDEX \"orchestration_logs_tenantId_createdAt_idx\" ON \"orchestration_logs\"(\"tenantId\", \"createdAt\");\nCREATE INDEX \"orchestration_logs_virtualUserId_idx\" ON \"orchestration_logs\"(\"virtualUserId\");\nCREATE INDEX \"orchestration_logs_intent_idx\" ON \"orchestration_logs\"(\"intent\");\n```\n\n### 4.4 Post-Migration Verification\n\n```bash\n# Verify table created\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\d orchestration_logs\"\n\n# Verify indexes\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"\\di orchestration_logs_*\"\n\n# Test inserting a log entry\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"INSERT INTO orchestration_logs (tenantId, virtualUserId, conversationId, intent, avatar, corpusWeight, userWeight) VALUES ('test', 'test', 'test', 'CORPUS_QUERY', 'STRANGER', 0.8, 0.2)\"\n```\n\n### 4.5 Rollback Plan\n\n```bash\n# Rollback migration\nnpx prisma migrate rollback --name add_orchestration_telemetry\n\n# Or manually drop table\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF\nDROP TABLE IF EXISTS \"orchestration_logs\" CASCADE;\nEOF\n```\n\n### 4.6 Success Criteria\n\n- [ ] Table created successfully\n- [ ] Indexes created\n- [ ] Can insert log entries\n- [ ] No impact on existing functionality\n\n---\n\n## Post-Migration Tasks\n\n### Database Optimization\n\n```sql\n-- Analyze all new tables for query optimization\nANALYZE tenants;\nANALYZE corpus_documents;\nANALYZE corpus_chunks;\nANALYZE corpus_topics;\nANALYZE conversation_indices;\nANALYZE user_profile_snapshots;\nANALYZE orchestration_logs;\n\n-- Verify pgvector indexes are working\nSELECT indexname, indexdef\nFROM pg_indexes\nWHERE tablename = 'corpus_chunks'\n  AND indexdef LIKE '%vector%';\n\n-- Check table sizes\nSELECT\n  relname AS table_name,\n  pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(relid)) AS total_size\nFROM pg_catalog.pg_statio_user_tables\nORDER BY pg_total_relation_size(relid) DESC;\n```\n\n### Application Deployment\n\n```bash\n# 1. Deploy updated Prisma Client\nnpx prisma generate\n\n# 2. Restart application servers\npm2 restart vivim-server\n\n# 3. Verify deployment\ncurl http://localhost:3000/api/health\n\n# 4. Run smoke tests\nnpm run test:smoke\n```\n\n### Monitoring Setup\n\n```typescript\n// Add monitoring for new tables\n// src/monitoring/corpus-metrics.ts\n\nimport { Metrics } from 'prom-client';\n\nconst corpusMetrics = {\n  corpusChunksTotal: new Metrics.Gauge({\n    name: 'corpus_chunks_total',\n    help: 'Total number of corpus chunks',\n  }),\n\n  corpusQueriesTotal: new Metrics.Counter({\n    name: 'corpus_queries_total',\n    help: 'Total corpus queries',\n    labelNames: ['tenantId', 'intent'],\n  }),\n\n  corpusAssemblyDuration: new Metrics.Histogram({\n    name: 'corpus_assembly_duration_seconds',\n    help: 'Corpus context assembly duration',\n    buckets: [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5],\n  }),\n\n  virtualUserAvatarDistribution: new Metrics.Gauge({\n    name: 'virtual_user_avatar_distribution',\n    help: 'Distribution of user avatars',\n    labelNames: ['avatar'],\n  }),\n};\n\n// Update metrics periodically\nsetInterval(async () => {\n  const chunkCount = await prisma.corpusChunk.count();\n  corpusMetrics.corpusChunksTotal.set(chunkCount);\n\n  const avatarCounts = await prisma.virtualUser.groupBy({\n    by: ['currentAvatar'],\n    _count: true,\n  });\n\n  for (const { currentAvatar, _count } of avatarCounts) {\n    corpusMetrics.virtualUserAvatarDistribution\n      .labels(currentAvatar)\n      .set(_count);\n  }\n}, 60000);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Rollback Decision Matrix\n\n| Issue | Severity | Rollback Required? | Mitigation |\n|-------|----------|-------------------|------------|\n| Migration SQL error | High | Yes | Fix SQL, re-run migration |\n| Index creation fails | Medium | No | Create indexes manually post-migration |\n| Foreign key constraint violation | High | Yes | Fix data integrity, re-run |\n| Application errors post-migration | Medium | Maybe | Fix application code, redeploy |\n| Performance degradation | Medium | No | Optimize queries, add indexes |\n| Data corruption | Critical | Yes | Restore from backup |\n\n---\n\n## Rollback Procedure (Full)\n\nIf a full rollback is required:\n\n```bash\n# 1. Stop application\npm2 stop vivim-server\n\n# 2. Restore database from backup\npg_restore -U vivim_user -h localhost -d vivim_db \\\n  --clean \\\n  --if-exists \\\n  vivim_backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump\n\n# 3. Verify restoration\npsql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM virtual_users\"\n\n# 4. Restart application\npm2 start vivim-server\n\n# 5. Verify application health\ncurl http://localhost:3000/api/health\n```\n\n---\n\n## Migration Timeline Summary\n\n| Phase | Migration Name | Duration | Risk | Rollback Time |\n|-------|---------------|----------|------|---------------|\n| 1 | `add_corpus_core_models` | 2-4 hours | Low | 30 min |\n| 2 | `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields` | 1-2 hours | Low | 15 min |\n| 3 | `add_conversation_awareness_models` | 2-3 hours | Low | 30 min |\n| 4 | `add_orchestration_telemetry` | 1 hour | Minimal | 10 min |\n\n**Total Migration Time:** 6-10 hours (can be spread across multiple days)  \n**Total Rollback Time:** ~2 hours (worst case)\n\n---\n\n## Success Criteria (Overall)\n\n- [ ] All 4 migrations applied successfully\n- [ ] No breaking changes to existing APIs\n- [ ] All existing queries still work\n- [ ] New tables have correct data\n- [ ] Vector indexes working correctly\n- [ ] Application health checks pass\n- [ ] Monitoring dashboards showing green\n- [ ] No increase in error rates\n- [ ] Performance within acceptable ranges\n\n---\n\n**Document Status:** Ready for Execution  \n**Approved By:** [Pending]  \n**Migration Start Date:** [TBD]\n",

````diff
# VIVIM Corpus-Chatbot Migration Strategy

**Document Purpose:** Detailed migration plan for gracefully integrating the corpus-chatbot system with zero downtime and backward compatibility.

**Created:** March 27, 2026  
**Migration Type:** Progressive, Non-Breaking  
**Risk Level:** Low

---

## Migration Overview

### Guiding Principles

1. **Zero Breaking Changes:** All existing queries, APIs, and functionality must continue working
2. **Progressive Rollout:** Migrate in phases with rollback capability at each step
3. **Backward Compatible:** New fields are nullable or have defaults
4. **Data Safety:** Full backups before each migration phase
5. **Monitoring:** Track migration progress and system health throughout

### Migration Timeline

```
Phase 1: Corpus Core Models      [Week 1]    ████████░░░░░░░░ 25%
Phase 2: VirtualUser Extensions  [Week 2]    ████████████░░░░ 50%
Phase 3: Conversation Awareness  [Week 3]    ████████████████ 75%
Phase 4: Telemetry + Polish      [Week 4]    ████████████████ 100%
```

---

## Pre-Migration Checklist

### Infrastructure Requirements

- [ ] PostgreSQL 14+ with pgvector extension installed
- [ ] Prisma CLI installed (`npx prisma --version`)
- [ ] Database backup strategy in place
- [ ] Staging environment available for testing
- [ ] Monitoring dashboards configured (Datadog, Grafana, etc.)

### Code Readiness

- [ ] All new TypeScript types defined
- [ ] Repository layer updated with tenant scoping
- [ ] API routes created for corpus operations
- [ ] Unit tests written for new services
- [ ] Integration tests passing in staging

### Database Backup

```bash
# Full database backup before migration
pg_dump -U vivim_user -h localhost vivim_db \
  --format=custom \
  --compress=9 \
  --file=vivim_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump

# Verify backup
pg_restore --list vivim_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).dump

# Store backup in secure location
aws s3 cp vivim_backup_*.dump s3://vivim-backups/pre-migration/
```

---

## Phase 1: Corpus Core Models Migration

**Migration Name:** `add_corpus_core_models`  
**Duration:** 2-4 hours (depends on data volume)  
**Risk:** Low (all new models, no existing changes)

### 1.1 Schema Changes

```prisma
// NEW MODELS (no existing models modified)
model Tenant { }
model CorpusDocument { }
model CorpusDocumentVersion { }
model CorpusChunk { }
model CorpusTopic { }
model CorpusFAQ { }

// NEW ENUMS
enum UserAvatar { STRANGER, ACQUAINTANCE, FAMILIAR, KNOWN }
enum TopicScope { USER, CORPUS }
enum ChunkContentType { prose, code, table, list, mixed }
enum DocumentChangeType { major, minor, patch }
enum OrchestrationIntent { ... }
enum ConversationSentiment { positive, neutral, negative, mixed }
enum ResolutionStatus { resolved, pending, escalated, unknown }
```

### 1.2 Migration Command

```bash
# Step 1: Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_corpus_core_models

# Step 2: Review generated SQL
cat prisma/migrations/*/migration.sql

# Step 3: Apply to production
npx prisma migrate deploy
```

### 1.3 Generated SQL (Preview)

```sql
-- Create enums
CREATE TYPE "UserAvatar" AS ENUM ('STRANGER', 'ACQUAINTANCE', 'FAMILIAR', 'KNOWN');
CREATE TYPE "TopicScope" AS ENUM ('USER', 'CORPUS');
CREATE TYPE "ChunkContentType" AS ENUM ('prose', 'code', 'table', 'list', 'mixed');
CREATE TYPE "DocumentChangeType" AS ENUM ('major', 'minor', 'patch');
CREATE TYPE "OrchestrationIntent" AS ENUM (...);
CREATE TYPE "ConversationSentiment" AS ENUM ('positive', 'neutral', 'negative', 'mixed');
CREATE TYPE "ResolutionStatus" AS ENUM ('resolved', 'pending', 'escalated', 'unknown');

-- Create tables
CREATE TABLE "tenants" (
  "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  "name" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "slug" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "chatbotConfig" JSONB,
  "brandVoice" JSONB,
  "guardrails" TEXT[],
  "defaultModel" TEXT DEFAULT 'gpt-4o',
  "embeddingModel" TEXT DEFAULT 'text-embedding-3-small',
  "maxContextTokens" INTEGER DEFAULT 12000,
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  "updatedAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

CREATE TABLE "corpus_documents" ( ... );
CREATE TABLE "corpus_document_versions" ( ... );
CREATE TABLE "corpus_chunks" ( ... );
CREATE TABLE "corpus_topics" ( ... );
CREATE TABLE "corpus_faqs" ( ... );

-- Create indexes
CREATE INDEX "tenants_slug_idx" ON "tenants"("slug");
CREATE INDEX "corpus_chunks_tenantId_embedding_idx" ON "corpus_chunks"("tenantId", "embedding");
-- ... more indexes

-- Enable pgvector (if not already enabled)
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
```

### 1.4 Post-Migration Verification

```bash
# Verify tables created
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\dt tenants corpus_*"

# Verify enums created
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\dT UserAvatar TopicScope"

# Verify indexes created
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\di tenants_* corpus_*"

# Run health check
curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
```

### 1.5 Rollback Plan

```bash
# Rollback migration
npx prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back add_corpus_core_models

# Or manually drop tables
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "corpus_faqs" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "corpus_chunks" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "corpus_topics" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "corpus_document_versions" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "corpus_documents" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "tenants" CASCADE;
DROP TYPE IF EXISTS "UserAvatar";
DROP TYPE IF EXISTS "TopicScope";
-- ... drop other enums
EOF
```

### 1.6 Success Criteria

- [ ] All tables created successfully
- [ ] All enums created successfully
- [ ] All indexes created successfully
- [ ] pgvector extension enabled
- [ ] No errors in migration logs
- [ ] Health check passes
- [ ] Existing queries unaffected

---

## Phase 2: VirtualUser Extensions Migration

**Migration Name:** `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields`  
**Duration:** 1-2 hours  
**Risk:** Low (nullable fields, backward compatible)

### 2.1 Schema Changes

```prisma
model VirtualUser {
  // ... EXISTING FIELDS ...

  // NEW: Tenant association (nullable, backward compatible)
  tenantId              String?
  tenant                Tenant? @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  // NEW: Avatar classification (with default)
  currentAvatar         String    @default("STRANGER")
  profileVersion        Int       @default(0)

  // NEW: Relations (additive, no breaking changes)
  conversationIndices   ConversationIndex[]
  profileSnapshots      UserProfileSnapshot[]
}
```

### 2.2 Migration Command

```bash
# Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields

# Apply to production
npx prisma migrate deploy
```

### 2.3 Generated SQL (Preview)

```sql
-- Add columns to existing VirtualUser table
ALTER TABLE "virtual_users"
  ADD COLUMN "tenantId" UUID,
  ADD COLUMN "currentAvatar" "UserAvatar" NOT NULL DEFAULT 'STRANGER',
  ADD COLUMN "profileVersion" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0;

-- Add foreign key constraint
ALTER TABLE "virtual_users"
  ADD CONSTRAINT "virtual_users_tenantId_fkey"
  FOREIGN KEY ("tenantId")
  REFERENCES "tenants"("id")
  ON DELETE SET NULL;

-- Create index for tenant lookup
CREATE INDEX "virtual_users_tenantId_idx" ON "virtual_users"("tenantId");

-- Create index for avatar classification
CREATE INDEX "virtual_users_currentAvatar_idx" ON "virtual_users"("currentAvatar");
```

### 2.4 Data Migration (Optional)

If you want to pre-populate `currentAvatar` based on existing data:

```sql
-- Update avatar based on existing conversation count
UPDATE "virtual_users" vu
SET "currentAvatar" = CASE
  WHEN vu."conversationCount" = 0 THEN 'STRANGER'
  WHEN vu."conversationCount" <= 5 THEN 'ACQUAINTANCE'
  WHEN vu."conversationCount" <= 20 THEN 'FAMILIAR'
  ELSE 'KNOWN'
END
WHERE "currentAvatar" = 'STRANGER';  -- Only update default values
```

### 2.5 Post-Migration Verification

```bash
# Verify columns added
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\d virtual_users"

# Verify foreign key
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\df virtual_users_tenantId_fkey"

# Test existing VirtualUser queries still work
curl http://localhost:3000/api/v1/virtual/profile?virtualUserId=test-uuid

# Verify new fields are nullable/defaults
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "SELECT id, tenantId, currentAvatar FROM virtual_users LIMIT 5"
```

### 2.6 Rollback Plan

```bash
# Rollback migration
npx prisma migrate rollback --name add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields

# Or manually revert
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF
ALTER TABLE "virtual_users"
  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "tenantId",
  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "currentAvatar",
  DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS "profileVersion";

DROP INDEX IF EXISTS "virtual_users_tenantId_idx";
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS "virtual_users_currentAvatar_idx";
EOF
```

### 2.7 Success Criteria

- [ ] Columns added successfully
- [ ] Foreign key constraint created
- [ ] Indexes created
- [ ] Existing VirtualUser queries work
- [ ] New fields have correct defaults
- [ ] No NULL constraint violations

---

## Phase 3: Conversation Awareness Models

**Migration Name:** `add_conversation_awareness_models`  
**Duration:** 2-3 hours  
**Risk:** Low (new companion models)

### 3.1 Schema Changes

```prisma
// NEW MODELS
model ConversationIndex {
  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())
  virtualUserId       String
  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(...)
  conversationId      String    @unique
  // ... other fields
}

model UserProfileSnapshot {
  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())
  virtualUserId       String
  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(...)
  avatar              String
  version             Int       @default(1)
  // ... other fields
}
```

### 3.2 Migration Command

```bash
# Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_conversation_awareness_models

# Apply to production
npx prisma migrate deploy
```

### 3.3 Generated SQL (Preview)

```sql
-- Create ConversationIndex table
CREATE TABLE "conversation_indices" (
  "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  "virtualUserId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "conversationId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "summary" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "embedding" vector(1536),
  "topics" TEXT[],
  "keyFacts" JSONB,
  "questionsAsked" TEXT[],
  "issuesDiscussed" TEXT[],
  "decisionsReached" TEXT[],
  "actionItems" TEXT[],
  "messageCount" INTEGER,
  "duration" INTEGER,
  "sentiment" "ConversationSentiment",
  "resolutionStatus" "ResolutionStatus",
  "startedAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),
  "endedAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),
  "lastReferencedAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),
  "referenceCount" INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  "relatedConversationIds" TEXT[],
  "memoryIds" TEXT[],
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"),
  CONSTRAINT "conversation_indices_conversationId_unique" UNIQUE ("conversationId"),
  CONSTRAINT "conversation_indices_virtualUserId_fkey"
    FOREIGN KEY ("virtualUserId")
    REFERENCES "virtual_users"("id")
    ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Create UserProfileSnapshot table
CREATE TABLE "user_profile_snapshots" (
  "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  "virtualUserId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "avatar" "UserAvatar" NOT NULL,
  "version" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
  "identity" JSONB,
  "preferences" JSONB,
  "topicExpertise" JSONB,
  "behavior" JSONB,
  "activeConcerns" JSONB,
  "evolutionLog" JSONB,
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  "lastEvolvedAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"),
  CONSTRAINT "user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_version_unique"
    UNIQUE ("virtualUserId", "version"),
  CONSTRAINT "user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_fkey"
    FOREIGN KEY ("virtualUserId")
    REFERENCES "virtual_users"("id")
    ON DELETE CASCADE
);

-- Create indexes
CREATE INDEX "conversation_indices_virtualUserId_startedAt_idx" ON "conversation_indices"("virtualUserId", "startedAt");
CREATE INDEX "conversation_indices_virtualUserId_embedding_idx" ON "conversation_indices"("virtualUserId", "embedding");
CREATE INDEX "conversation_indices_virtualUserId_topics_idx" ON "conversation_indices"("virtualUserId", "topics");
CREATE INDEX "user_profile_snapshots_virtualUserId_idx" ON "user_profile_snapshots"("virtualUserId");
```

### 3.4 Post-Migration Verification

```bash
# Verify tables created
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\d conversation_indices user_profile_snapshots"

# Verify unique constraint on conversationId
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\d conversation_indices"

# Verify vector column
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'conversation_indices' AND column_name = 'embedding'"

# Test existing Conversation queries still work
curl http://localhost:3000/api/v1/virtual/conversations?virtualUserId=test-uuid
```

### 3.5 Rollback Plan

```bash
# Rollback migration
npx prisma migrate rollback --name add_conversation_awareness_models

# Or manually drop tables
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "user_profile_snapshots" CASCADE;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "conversation_indices" CASCADE;
EOF
```

### 3.6 Success Criteria

- [ ] Tables created successfully
- [ ] Foreign key constraints created
- [ ] Unique constraint on conversationId
- [ ] Vector column created
- [ ] Indexes created
- [ ] Existing Conversation queries work
- [ ] No cascade delete issues

## Phase 4: Telemetry + Final Polish

**Migration Name:** `add_orchestration_telemetry`  
**Duration:** 1 hour  
**Risk:** Minimal (pure telemetry, no existing model changes)

### 4.1 Schema Changes

```prisma
// NEW MODEL
model OrchestrationLog {
  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())
  tenantId            String
  virtualUserId       String
  conversationId      String
  // ... telemetry fields
}
```

### 4.2 Migration Command

```bash
# Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_orchestration_telemetry

# Apply to production
npx prisma migrate deploy
```

### 4.3 Generated SQL (Preview)

```sql
-- Create OrchestrationLog table
CREATE TABLE "orchestration_logs" (
  "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  "tenantId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "virtualUserId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "conversationId" UUID NOT NULL,
  "intent" "OrchestrationIntent" NOT NULL,
  "intentConfidence" DOUBLE PRECISION,
  "avatar" "UserAvatar" NOT NULL,
  "corpusWeight" DOUBLE PRECISION,
  "userWeight" DOUBLE PRECISION,
  "assemblyTimeMs" INTEGER,
  "totalTokens" INTEGER,
  "corpusTokens" INTEGER,
  "userTokens" INTEGER,
  "historyTokens" INTEGER,
  "corpusConfidence" DOUBLE PRECISION,
  "chunksRetrieved" INTEGER,
  "memoriesUsed" INTEGER,
  "proactiveInsights" INTEGER,
  "userSatisfaction" TEXT,
  "followUpRequired" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  "escalated" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  "createdAt" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- Create indexes
CREATE INDEX "orchestration_logs_tenantId_createdAt_idx" ON "orchestration_logs"("tenantId", "createdAt");
CREATE INDEX "orchestration_logs_virtualUserId_idx" ON "orchestration_logs"("virtualUserId");
CREATE INDEX "orchestration_logs_intent_idx" ON "orchestration_logs"("intent");
```

### 4.4 Post-Migration Verification

```bash
# Verify table created
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\d orchestration_logs"

# Verify indexes
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "\di orchestration_logs_*"

# Test inserting a log entry
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "INSERT INTO orchestration_logs (tenantId, virtualUserId, conversationId, intent, avatar, corpusWeight, userWeight) VALUES ('test', 'test', 'test', 'CORPUS_QUERY', 'STRANGER', 0.8, 0.2)"
```

### 4.5 Rollback Plan

```bash
# Rollback migration
npx prisma migrate rollback --name add_orchestration_telemetry

# Or manually drop table
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db <<EOF
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS "orchestration_logs" CASCADE;
EOF
```

- [ ] Table created successfully
- [ ] Indexes created
- [ ] Can insert log entries

## Post-Migration Tasks

### Database Optimization

```sql
-- Analyze all new tables for query optimization
ANALYZE tenants;
ANALYZE corpus_documents;
ANALYZE corpus_chunks;
ANALYZE corpus_topics;
ANALYZE conversation_indices;
ANALYZE user_profile_snapshots;
ANALYZE orchestration_logs;

-- Verify pgvector indexes are working
SELECT indexname, indexdef
FROM pg_indexes
WHERE tablename = 'corpus_chunks'
  AND indexdef LIKE '%vector%';

-- Check table sizes
SELECT
  relname AS table_name,
  pg_size_pretty(pg_total_relation_size(relid)) AS total_size
FROM pg_catalog.pg_statio_user_tables
ORDER BY pg_total_relation_size(relid) DESC;
```

### Application Deployment

```bash
# 1. Deploy updated Prisma Client
npx prisma generate

# 2. Restart application servers
pm2 restart vivim-server

# 3. Verify deployment
curl http://localhost:3000/api/health

# 4. Run smoke tests
npm run test:smoke
```

### Monitoring Setup

```typescript
// Add monitoring for new tables
// src/monitoring/corpus-metrics.ts

import { Metrics } from 'prom-client';

const corpusMetrics = {
  corpusChunksTotal: new Metrics.Gauge({
    name: 'corpus_chunks_total',
    help: 'Total number of corpus chunks',
  }),

  corpusQueriesTotal: new Metrics.Counter({
    name: 'corpus_queries_total',
    help: 'Total corpus queries',
    labelNames: ['tenantId', 'intent'],
  }),

  corpusAssemblyDuration: new Metrics.Histogram({
    name: 'corpus_assembly_duration_seconds',
    help: 'Corpus context assembly duration',
    buckets: [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5],
  }),

  virtualUserAvatarDistribution: new Metrics.Gauge({
    name: 'virtual_user_avatar_distribution',
    help: 'Distribution of user avatars',
    labelNames: ['avatar'],
  }),
};

// Update metrics periodically
setInterval(async () => {
  const chunkCount = await prisma.corpusChunk.count();
  corpusMetrics.corpusChunksTotal.set(chunkCount);

  const avatarCounts = await prisma.virtualUser.groupBy({
    by: ['currentAvatar'],
    _count: true,
  });

  for (const { currentAvatar, _count } of avatarCounts) {
    corpusMetrics.virtualUserAvatarDistribution
      .labels(currentAvatar)
      .set(_count);
  }
}, 60000);
```

---

## Rollback Decision Matrix

| Issue | Severity | Rollback Required? | Mitigation |
|-------|----------|-------------------|------------|
| Migration SQL error | High | Yes | Fix SQL, re-run migration |
| Index creation fails | Medium | No | Create indexes manually post-migration |
| Foreign key constraint violation | High | Yes | Fix data integrity, re-run |
| Application errors post-migration | Medium | Maybe | Fix application code, redeploy |
| Performance degradation | Medium | No | Optimize queries, add indexes |
| Data corruption | Critical | Yes | Restore from backup |

---

## Rollback Procedure (Full)

If a full rollback is required:

```bash
# 1. Stop application
pm2 stop vivim-server

# 2. Restore database from backup
pg_restore -U vivim_user -h localhost -d vivim_db \
  --clean \
  --if-exists \
  vivim_backup_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS.dump

# 3. Verify restoration
psql -U vivim_user -d vivim_db -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM virtual_users"

# 4. Restart application
pm2 start vivim-server

# 5. Verify application health
curl http://localhost:3000/api/health
```

---

## Migration Timeline Summary

| Phase | Migration Name | Duration | Risk | Rollback Time |
|-------|---------------|----------|------|---------------|
| 1 | `add_corpus_core_models` | 2-4 hours | Low | 30 min |
| 2 | `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields` | 1-2 hours | Low | 15 min |
| 3 | `add_conversation_awareness_models` | 2-3 hours | Low | 30 min |
| 4 | `add_orchestration_telemetry` | 1 hour | Minimal | 10 min |

**Total Migration Time:** 6-10 hours (can be spread across multiple days)  
**Total Rollback Time:** ~2 hours (worst case)

---

- [ ] Application health checks pass
````

````json
{
  "content": "# VIVIM Corpus-Chatbot Integration\n\n**Documentation Readiness Package**\n\nThis folder contains the complete integration plan for adding the dual-engine corpus chatbot system to the existing VIVIM memory and context infrastructure.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Navigation\n\n| Document | Purpose | Status |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| [01-schema-integration-mapping.md](./01-schema-integration-mapping.md) | Schema comparison and integration mapping | ✅ Complete |\n| [02-implementation-plan.md](./02-implementation-plan.md) | 8-week detailed implementation roadmap | ✅ Complete |\n| [03-migration-strategy.md](./03-migration-strategy.md) | Progressive migration with zero downtime | ✅ Complete |\n\n---\n\n## Executive Summary\n\n### What We're Building\n\nA **company chatbot system** that:\n\n1. **Answers from company documentation** using a Corpus Context Engine (C0-C4 layers)\n2. **Remembers every visitor** without requiring login via Virtual User Identification\n3. **Blends both intelligently** using a Dual-Engine Orchestrator\n4. **Makes users feel recognized** with Intelligent User Memory and conversation awareness\n\n### Architecture Overview\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    COMPANY CHATBOT WIDGET                        │\n└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘\n                             │\n                             ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              DUAL-ENGINE ORCHESTRATOR                            │\n│                                                                  │\n│  Intent Classifier → Weight Calculator → Budget Allocator       │\n│                                                                  │\n│         ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐            │\n│         │  USER CONTEXT    │    │  CORPUS CONTEXT  │            │\n│         │  (L0-L7)         │    │  (C0-C4)         │            │\n│         │  Existing VIVIM  │    │  New System      │            │\n│         └────────┬─────────┘    └────────┬─────────┘            │\n│                  └──────────┬─────────────┘                     │\n│                             ▼                                   │\n│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │\n│                  │  CONTEXT MERGER  │                          │\n│                  └────────┬─────────┘                          │\n│                           ▼                                     │\n│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │\n│                  │  MERGED PROMPT   │                          │\n│                  └────────┬─────────┘                          │\n│                           ▼                                     │\n│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │\n│                  │  LLM INFERENCE   │                          │\n│                  └──────────────────┘                          │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Key Features\n\n| Feature | Description | Status |\n|---------|-------------|--------|\n| **Corpus Engine** | 5-layer context system (C0-C4) for company knowledge | Ready to implement |\n| **Dual-Engine Orchestration** | Dynamic blending of corpus + user context | Ready to implement |\n| **Intent Classification** | Rule-based + LLM classification for engine weighting | Ready to implement |\n| **Conversation Awareness** | Full conversation indexing for recall | Ready to implement |\n| **Progressive Memory** | Real-time + session-end + consolidation extraction | Ready to implement |\n| **User Profile Evolution** | Avatar-based maturity tracking (STRANGER → KNOWN) | Ready to implement |\n| **Proactive Awareness** | Doc update detection, feature release matching | Ready to implement |\n| **Multi-Tenant** | Company-specific deployments with isolation | Ready to implement |\n\n---\n\n## Schema Integration Summary\n\n### New Models (9)\n\n| Model | Purpose | Integration Risk |\n|-------|---------|------------------|\n| `Tenant` | Multi-tenant company deployment | ✅ None (new) |\n| `CorpusDocument` | Company document storage | ✅ None (new) |\n| `CorpusDocumentVersion` | Document versioning | ✅ None (new) |\n| `CorpusChunk` | Chunked content with embeddings | ✅ None (new) |\n| `CorpusTopic` | Topic taxonomy | ✅ None (new) |\n| `CorpusFAQ` | FAQ pairs | ✅ None (new) |\n| `ConversationIndex` | Conversation summary index | ✅ None (new) |\n| `UserProfileSnapshot` | Evolving user profile | ✅ None (new) |\n| `OrchestrationLog` | Telemetry | ✅ None (new) |\n\n### Extended Models (2)\n\n| Model | Extension | Backward Compatible |\n|-------|-----------|---------------------|\n| `VirtualUser` | Add `tenantId`, `currentAvatar`, `profileVersion` | ✅ Yes (nullable fields) |\n| `Conversation` | Optional tenant link (via metadata) | ✅ Yes (optional) |\n\n### New Enums (7)\n\n- `UserAvatar` - STRANGER, ACQUAINTANCE, FAMILIAR, KNOWN\n- `TopicScope` - USER, CORPUS\n- `ChunkContentType` - prose, code, table, list, mixed\n- `DocumentChangeType` - major, minor, patch\n- `OrchestrationIntent` - 13 intent types\n- `ConversationSentiment` - positive, neutral, negative, mixed\n- `ResolutionStatus` - resolved, pending, escalated, unknown\n\n**Schema Compatibility:** ✅ EXCELLENT - Zero breaking changes\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Timeline\n\n### 8-Week Roadmap\n\n```\nWeek 1-2:  Corpus Engine Foundation     [████████░░░░░░░░] 25%\nWeek 3-4:  Dual-Engine Orchestrator     [████████████████] 50%\nWeek 5-6:  Intelligent User Memory      [████████████████] 75%\nWeek 7-8:  Polish + Multi-Tenant        [████████████████] 100%\n```\n\n### Phase Deliverables\n\n| Phase | Duration | Deliverables |\n|-------|----------|--------------|\n| **Week 1-2** | Corpus Foundation | Schema, parsers, chunker, embedding, retrieval, caching |\n| **Week 3-4** | Orchestrator | Intent classifier, weight calculator, budget allocator, context merger |\n| **Week 5-6** | User Memory | Conversation index, recall, progressive extraction, profile evolution |\n| **Week 7-8** | Production | Multi-tenant, analytics, rate limiting, load testing, docs |\n\n**Total Effort:** ~320 engineering hours\n\n---\n\n## Migration Strategy\n\n### 4-Phase Progressive Migration\n\n| Phase | Migration | Duration | Risk |\n|-------|-----------|----------|------|\n| 1 | `add_corpus_core_models` | 2-4 hours | Low |\n| 2 | `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields` | 1-2 hours | Low |\n| 3 | `add_conversation_awareness_models` | 2-3 hours | Low |\n| 4 | `add_orchestration_telemetry` | 1 hour | Minimal |\n\n**Total Migration Time:** 6-10 hours (can be spread across days)  \n**Rollback Time:** ~2 hours (worst case)  \n**Downtime:** ZERO (progressive, backward compatible)\n\n---\n\n## Success Metrics\n\n| Metric | Target | Measurement |\n|--------|--------|-------------|\n| **Assembly Time** | < 800ms | P95 latency |\n| **Corpus Confidence** | > 0.6 avg | Avg confidence score |\n| **Identification Rate** | > 85% | Returning users identified |\n| **Memory Density** | > 5/week | Memories created per user |\n| **Cache Hit Rate** | > 70% | Corpus cache hits |\n| **User Satisfaction** | > 4.0/5.0 | Thumbs up rate |\n\n---\n\n## Getting Started\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- [ ] PostgreSQL 14+ with pgvector extension\n- [ ] Prisma CLI installed\n- [ ] Node.js 20+\n- [ ] OpenAI API key (for embeddings + LLM)\n- [ ] Redis (optional, for L2 cache)\n\n### Quick Start\n\n```bash\n# 1. *Diff for `README.md`:*
````
````diff
# VIVIM Corpus-Chatbot Integration

**Documentation Readiness Package**

This folder contains the complete integration plan for adding the dual-engine corpus chatbot system to the existing VIVIM memory and context infrastructure.

---

## Quick Navigation

| Document | Purpose | Status |
|----------|---------|--------|
| [01-schema-integration-mapping.md](./01-schema-integration-mapping.md) | Schema comparison and integration mapping | ✅ Complete |
| [02-implementation-plan.md](./02-implementation-plan.md) | 8-week detailed implementation roadmap | ✅ Complete |
| [03-migration-strategy.md](./03-migration-strategy.md) | Progressive migration with zero downtime | ✅ Complete |

---

## Executive Summary

### What We're Building

A **company chatbot system** that:

1. **Answers from company documentation** using a Corpus Context Engine (C0-C4 layers)
2. **Remembers every visitor** without requiring login via Virtual User Identification
3. **Blends both intelligently** using a Dual-Engine Orchestrator
4. **Makes users feel recognized** with Intelligent User Memory and conversation awareness

### Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    COMPANY CHATBOT WIDGET                        │
└────────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
                             ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              DUAL-ENGINE ORCHESTRATOR                            │
│                                                                  │
│  Intent Classifier → Weight Calculator → Budget Allocator       │
│                                                                  │
│         ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐            │
│         │  USER CONTEXT    │    │  CORPUS CONTEXT  │            │
│         │  (L0-L7)         │    │  (C0-C4)         │            │
│         │  Existing VIVIM  │    │  New System      │            │
│         └────────┬─────────┘    └────────┬─────────┘            │
│                  └──────────┬─────────────┘                     │
│                             ▼                                   │
│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │
│                  │  CONTEXT MERGER  │                          │
│                  └────────┬─────────┘                          │
│                           ▼                                     │
│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │
│                  │  MERGED PROMPT   │                          │
│                  └────────┬─────────┘                          │
│                           ▼                                     │
│                  ┌──────────────────┐                          │
│                  │  LLM INFERENCE   │                          │
│                  └──────────────────┘                          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Key Features

| Feature | Description | Status |
|---------|-------------|--------|
| **Corpus Engine** | 5-layer context system (C0-C4) for company knowledge | Ready to implement |
| **Dual-Engine Orchestration** | Dynamic blending of corpus + user context | Ready to implement |
| **Intent Classification** | Rule-based + LLM classification for engine weighting | Ready to implement |
| **Conversation Awareness** | Full conversation indexing for recall | Ready to implement |
| **Progressive Memory** | Real-time + session-end + consolidation extraction | Ready to implement |
| **User Profile Evolution** | Avatar-based maturity tracking (STRANGER → KNOWN) | Ready to implement |
| **Proactive Awareness** | Doc update detection, feature release matching | Ready to implement |
| **Multi-Tenant** | Company-specific deployments with isolation | Ready to implement |

---

## Schema Integration Summary

### New Models (9)

| Model | Purpose | Integration Risk |
|-------|---------|------------------|
| `Tenant` | Multi-tenant company deployment | ✅ None (new) |
| `CorpusDocument` | Company document storage | ✅ None (new) |
| `CorpusDocumentVersion` | Document versioning | ✅ None (new) |
| `CorpusChunk` | Chunked content with embeddings | ✅ None (new) |
| `CorpusTopic` | Topic taxonomy | ✅ None (new) |
| `CorpusFAQ` | FAQ pairs | ✅ None (new) |
| `ConversationIndex` | Conversation summary index | ✅ None (new) |
| `UserProfileSnapshot` | Evolving user profile | ✅ None (new) |
| `OrchestrationLog` | Telemetry | ✅ None (new) |

### Extended Models (2)

| Model | Extension | Backward Compatible |
|-------|-----------|---------------------|
| `VirtualUser` | Add `tenantId`, `currentAvatar`, `profileVersion` | ✅ Yes (nullable fields) |
| `Conversation` | Optional tenant link (via metadata) | ✅ Yes (optional) |

### New Enums (7)

- `UserAvatar` - STRANGER, ACQUAINTANCE, FAMILIAR, KNOWN
- `TopicScope` - USER, CORPUS
- `ChunkContentType` - prose, code, table, list, mixed
- `DocumentChangeType` - major, minor, patch
- `OrchestrationIntent` - 13 intent types
- `ConversationSentiment` - positive, neutral, negative, mixed
- `ResolutionStatus` - resolved, pending, escalated, unknown

**Schema Compatibility:** ✅ EXCELLENT - Zero breaking changes

---

## Implementation Timeline

### 8-Week Roadmap

```
Week 1-2:  Corpus Engine Foundation     [████████░░░░░░░░] 25%
Week 3-4:  Dual-Engine Orchestrator     [████████████████] 50%
Week 5-6:  Intelligent User Memory      [████████████████] 75%
Week 7-8:  Polish + Multi-Tenant        [████████████████] 100%
```

### Phase Deliverables

| Phase | Duration | Deliverables |
|-------|----------|--------------|
| **Week 1-2** | Corpus Foundation | Schema, parsers, chunker, embedding, retrieval, caching |
| **Week 3-4** | Orchestrator | Intent classifier, weight calculator, budget allocator, context merger |
| **Week 5-6** | User Memory | Conversation index, recall, progressive extraction, profile evolution |
| **Week 7-8** | Production | Multi-tenant, analytics, rate limiting, load testing, docs |

**Total Effort:** ~320 engineering hours

---

## Migration Strategy

### 4-Phase Progressive Migration

| Phase | Migration | Duration | Risk |
|-------|-----------|----------|------|
| 1 | `add_corpus_core_models` | 2-4 hours | Low |
| 2 | `add_virtual_user_chatbot_fields` | 1-2 hours | Low |
| 3 | `add_conversation_awareness_models` | 2-3 hours | Low |
| 4 | `add_orchestration_telemetry` | 1 hour | Minimal |

**Total Migration Time:** 6-10 hours (can be spread across days)  
**Rollback Time:** ~2 hours (worst case)  
**Downtime:** ZERO (progressive, backward compatible)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| **Assembly Time** | < 800ms | P95 latency |
| **Corpus Confidence** | > 0.6 avg | Avg confidence score |
| **Identification Rate** | > 85% | Returning users identified |
| **Memory Density** | > 5/week | Memories created per user |
| **Cache Hit Rate** | > 70% | Corpus cache hits |
| **User Satisfaction** | > 4.0/5.0 | Thumbs up rate |

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- [ ] PostgreSQL 14+ with pgvector extension
- [ ] Prisma CLI installed
- [ ] Node.js 20+
- [ ] OpenAI API key (for embeddings + LLM)
- [ ] Redis (optional, for L2 cache)

### Quick Start

```bash
# 1. Clone repository
cd server

# 2. Install dependencies
bun install

# 3. Set environment variables
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your configuration

# 4. Run first migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_corpus_core_models

# 5. Generate Prisma Client
npx prisma generate

# 6. Start development server
bun run dev
```

### Environment Variables

```env
# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/vivim

# Corpus
CORPUS_DEFAULT_MODEL=gpt-4o
CORPUS_EMBEDDING_MODEL=text-embedding-3-small
CORPUS_MAX_CONTEXT_TOKENS=12000

# Virtual User
VIRTUAL_USER_ENABLED=true
VIRTUAL_USER_CONSENT_REQUIRED=true
VIRTUAL_USER_DEFAULT_RETENTION_POLICY=90_days

# OpenAI
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# Redis (optional)
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
```

---
````

## Summary

I've created a complete **Documentation Readiness Package** in the folder:
`server/docs/chatbot-corpus-integration/`

### Documents Created (4 files):

1. **README.md** - Overview and quick navigation
   - Executive summary
   - Architecture diagram
   - Quick start guide
   - Success metrics

2. **01-schema-integration-mapping.md** - Detailed schema comparison
   - Mapped all 9 new models from chatbot doc to existing VIVIM schema
   - Identified 2 models requiring extension (VirtualUser, Conversation)
   - Created 7 new enum definitions
   - **Result:** ✅ EXCELLENT compatibility - zero breaking changes

3. **02-implementation-plan.md** - 8-week detailed roadmap
   - Week-by-week task breakdown
   - Day-by-day implementation details
   - Code examples for all major components
   - Success criteria for each phase
   - **Total effort:** ~320 engineering hours

4. **03-migration-strategy.md** - Progressive 4-phase migration
   - Phase 1: Corpus Core Models (2-4 hours)
   - Phase 2: VirtualUser Extensions (1-2 hours)
   - Phase 3: Conversation Awareness (2-3 hours)
   - Phase 4: Telemetry (1 hour)
   - **Total:** 6-10 hours, zero downtime, full rollback capability

### Key Findings:

**Schema Compatibility:** The chatbot corpus schema integrates gracefully with the existing 2787-line VIVIM schema:
- All new models use consistent naming conventions
- Leverages existing pgvector infrastructure
- Backward compatible extensions (nullable fields)
- No breaking changes to existing queries

**Architecture:** The dual-engine system adds:
- **Corpus Context Engine (C0-C4)** - Company knowledge base
- **Dual-Engine Orchestrator** - Blends corpus + user context
- **Conversation Awareness** - Full conversation indexing
- **Proactive Memory** - Makes users feel recognized

**Integration Points:**
- Existing VIVIM Memory Engine → User context (L0-L7)
- New Corpus Engine → Company knowledge (C0-C4)
- Orchestrator dynamically weights both based on intent and user maturity

All documentation is ready for the implementation team to begin Week 1.

```json
{
  "content": "// ============================================================================\n// VIVIM Corpus-Chatbot Schema Extensions\n// ============================================================================\n// Purpose: Multi-tenant company chatbot with dual-engine orchestration\n// Created: March 27, 2026\n// \n// This schema extends the main VIVIM schema with:\n// - Tenant management for multi-tenant deployments\n// - Corpus document storage and chunking\n// - Topic taxonomy for company knowledge\n// - FAQ pairs for Q&A matching\n// - Conversation indexing for recall\n// - User profile snapshots for evolution tracking\n// - Orchestration telemetry\n// ============================================================================\n\n// ============================================================================\n// ENUMS\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * UserAvatar - Relationship maturity classification\n * Used by Dual-Engine Orchestrator to weight user vs corpus context\n */\nenum UserAvatar {\n  STRANGER      // First visit, 0 memories, 0 conversations\n  ACQUAINTANCE  // 2-5 visits, 1-10 memories, 1-5 conversations\n  FAMILIAR      // 6-20 visits, 11-50 memories, 6-20 conversations\n  KNOWN         // 20+ visits, 50+ memories, 20+ conversations\n}\n\n/**\n * TopicScope - Distinguish user topics from corpus topics\n */\nenum TopicScope {\n  USER    // User-specific interests (existing TopicProfile)\n  CORPUS  // Company knowledge taxonomy (new CorpusTopic)\n}\n\n/**\n * ChunkContentType - Type of content in a corpus chunk\n */\nenum ChunkContentType {\n  prose   // Regular text content\n  code    // Code blocks\n  table   // Data tables\n  list    // List items\n  mixed   // Combination of types\n}\n\n/**\n * DocumentChangeType - Semantic versioning change classification\n */\nenum DocumentChangeType {\n  major   // Breaking changes, major features\n  minor   // New features, backward compatible\n  patch   // Bug fixes, minor updates\n}\n\n/**\n * OrchestrationIntent - User query intent classification\n * Determines corpus vs user context weighting\n */\nenum OrchestrationIntent {\n  CORPUS_QUERY        // General product/doc question\n  CORPUS_DEEP_DIVE    // Detailed technical question\n  SUPPORT_QUERY       // Issue/problem needing help\n  USER_RECALL         // Referencing past interactions\n  COMPARISON          // Comparing features/options\n  HOW_TO              // Step-by-step guidance\n  STATUS_CHECK        // Checking on previous request\n  GENERAL_CHAT        // Casual conversation\n  FEEDBACK            // User providing feedback\n  ACCOUNT_SPECIFIC    // Questions about their situation\n  CLARIFICATION       // Follow-up to previous answer\n  NAVIGATION          // \"Where can I find X?\"\n  ESCALATION          // \"Let me talk to a human\"\n}\n\n/**\n * ConversationSentiment - Emotional tone of conversation\n */\nenum ConversationSentiment {\n  positive\n  neutral\n  negative\n  mixed\n}\n\n/**\n * ResolutionStatus - Issue resolution state\n */\nenum ResolutionStatus {\n  resolved\n  pending\n  escalated\n  unknown\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// TENANT MANAGEMENT\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * Tenant - Multi-tenant company deployment\n * Each tenant represents a company deploying the chatbot\n */\nmodel Tenant {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  name                String\n  slug                String    @unique\n\n  // Chatbot configuration (stored as JSON for flexibility)\n  chatbotConfig       Json      @db.JsonB  // CompanyIdentityCore config\n  brandVoice          Json      @db.JsonB  // BrandVoiceConfig\n  guardrails          String[]             // Things bot should NEVER say\n\n  // Corpus settings\n  defaultModel        String    @default(\"gpt-4o\")\n  embeddingModel      String    @default(\"text-embedding-3-small\")\n  maxContextTokens    Int       @default(12000)\n\n  // Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  updatedAt           DateTime  @updatedAt @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  // Relations\n  documents           CorpusDocument[]\n  topics              CorpusTopic[]\n  chunks              CorpusChunk[]\n  faqPairs            CorpusFAQ[]\n  virtualUsers        VirtualUser[]\n  orchestrationLogs   OrchestrationLog[]\n\n  @@index([slug])\n  @@map(\"tenants\")\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// CORPUS DOCUMENTS\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * CorpusDocument - Company document storage\n * Raw documents ingested into the corpus\n */\nmodel CorpusDocument {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  tenantId            String\n  tenant              Tenant    @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  // Content\n  title               String\n  description         String?\n  sourceUrl           String?             // Original doc URL\n  rawContent          String              @db.Text\n  contentHash         String              // SHA-256 for change detection\n  format              String              // markdown, html, pdf, api_spec\n\n  // Organization\n  category            String              // product_docs, api_reference, faq, etc.\n  topicId             String?\n  topic               CorpusTopic?        @relation(fields: [topicId], references: [id])\n\n  // Versioning\n  version             String              // \"2.3.1\"\n  isActive            Boolean             @default(true)\n  previousVersionId   String?\n\n  // Metadata\n  authors             String[]\n  lastPublishedAt     DateTime\n  wordCount           Int\n\n  // Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  updatedAt           DateTime  @updatedAt @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  ingestedAt          DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  // Relations\n  chunks              CorpusChunk[]\n  versions            CorpusDocumentVersion[]\n\n  @@index([tenantId, category])\n  @@index([tenantId, topicId])\n  @@index([contentHash])\n  @@map(\"corpus_documents\")\n}\n\n/**\n * CorpusDocumentVersion - Document version tracking\n * Tracks changes between document versions\n */\nmodel CorpusDocumentVersion {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  documentId          String\n  document            CorpusDocument @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  version             String\n  contentHash         String\n  changeType          String              @default(\"patch\") // major, minor, patch\n  changelog           String?             @db.Text // LLM-generated summary of changes\n  changedSections     String[]\n\n  addedChunkIds       String[]\n  modifiedChunkIds    String[]\n  removedChunkIds     String[]\n\n  isActive            Boolean   @default(false)\n  publishedAt         DateTime\n  ingestedAt          DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  @@index([documentId, version])\n  @@map(\"corpus_document_versions\")\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// CORPUS CHUNKS\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * CorpusChunk - Chunked document content with embeddings\n * The fundamental retrieval unit for the corpus\n */\nmodel CorpusChunk {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  tenantId            String\n  tenant              Tenant    @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  documentId          String\n  document            CorpusDocument @relation(fields: [documentId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  // Content\n  content             String    @db.Text\n  summary             String?             // 1-line LLM summary\n\n  // Hierarchy\n  parentChunkId       String?\n  parentChunk         CorpusChunk?        @relation(\"ChunkHierarchy\", fields: [parentChunkId], references: [id])\n  childChunks         CorpusChunk[]       @relation(\"ChunkHierarchy\")\n  chunkIndex          Int                 // Position in document\n  totalChunks         Int\n\n  // Structure\n  sectionPath         String[]            // [\"API Reference\", \"Auth\", \"OAuth\"]\n  headingLevel        Int                 // H1=1, H2=2, etc.\n  contentType         String              @default(\"prose\") // prose, code, table, list, mixed\n\n  // Embeddings\n  embedding           Unsupported(\"vector(1536)\")?\n  embeddingModel      String?\n\n  // Metadata\n  keywords            String[]\n  topicSlug           String\n  difficulty          String              @default(\"intermediate\") // beginner, intermediate, advanced\n\n  // Generated Q&A\n  generatedQuestions  String[]\n  generatedAnswer     String?             @db.Text\n  questionEmbeddings  Json?               @db.JsonB // Embeddings for each generated question\n\n  // Freshness\n  sourceUpdatedAt     DateTime\n  freshnessScore      Float   @default(1.0)\n\n  // Quality\n  qualityScore        Float   @default(0.5)\n\n  // Analytics\n  retrievalCount      Int     @default(0)\n  avgRelevanceScore   Float   @default(0.0)\n  lastRetrievedAt     DateTime?\n\n  // Status\n  isActive            Boolean @default(true)\n\n  // Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  updatedAt           DateTime  @updatedAt @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  // FAQ relations\n  faqPairs            CorpusFAQ[]\n\n  @@index([tenantId, topicSlug])\n  @@index([tenantId, contentType])\n  @@index([documentId, chunkIndex])\n  @@index([tenantId, embedding])\n  @@index([isActive])\n  @@map(\"corpus_chunks\")\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// CORPUS TOPICS\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * CorpusTopic - Company knowledge topic taxonomy\n * Hierarchical organization of company knowledge\n */\nmodel CorpusTopic {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  tenantId            String\n  tenant              Tenant    @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  slug                String\n  name                String\n  description         String?   @db.Text\n\n  // Hierarchy\n  parentTopicId       String?\n  parentTopic         CorpusTopic?  @relation(\"TopicHierarchy\", fields: [parentTopicId], references: [id])\n  childTopics         CorpusTopic[] @relation(\"TopicHierarchy\")\n  path                String[]\n  depth               Int       @default(0)\n\n  // Content stats\n  documentCount       Int       @default(0)\n  chunkCount          Int       @default(0)\n  totalTokens         Int       @default(0)\n\n  // Embeddings\n  embedding           Unsupported(\"vector(1536)\")?\n  representativeQuestions String[]\n\n  // Analytics\n  queryCount          Int       @default(0)\n  avgConfidence       Float     @default(0.0)\n  popularity          Float     @default(0.0)\n\n  // Freshness\n  lastUpdatedAt       DateTime  @default(now())\n\n  // Status\n  isActive            Boolean   @default(true)\n\n  // Relations\n  documents           CorpusDocument[]\n\n  @@unique([tenantId, slug])\n  @@index([tenantId, parentTopicId])\n  @@index([tenantId, embedding])\n  @@map(\"corpus_topics\")\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// CORPUS FAQ\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * CorpusFAQ - FAQ pairs for Q&A matching\n * Auto-generated or manually curated question/answer pairs\n */\nmodel CorpusFAQ {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  tenantId            String\n  tenant              Tenant    @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  question            String    @db.Text\n  answer              String    @db.Text\n\n  // Source\n  sourceChunkId       String?\n  sourceChunk         CorpusChunk? @relation(fields: [sourceChunkId], references: [id])\n  isManual            Boolean   @default(false)  // Manually curated vs auto-generated\n\n  // Embeddings\n  questionEmbedding   Unsupported(\"vector(1536)\")?\n\n  // Metadata\n  topicSlug           String\n  category            String\n  confidence          Float     @default(0.8)\n\n  // Analytics\n  matchCount          Int       @default(0)\n  helpfulCount        Int       @default(0)\n  unhelpfulCount      Int       @default(0)\n\n  // Status\n  isActive            Boolean   @default(true)\n\n  // Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  updatedAt           DateTime  @updatedAt @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  @@index([tenantId, topicSlug])\n  @@index([tenantId, questionEmbedding])\n  @@map(\"corpus_faqs\")\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// CONVERSATION AWARENESS\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * ConversationIndex - Searchable conversation summaries\n * Enables semantic search across past conversations for recall\n */\nmodel ConversationIndex {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  virtualUserId       String\n  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(fields: [virtualUserId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  conversationId      String    @unique\n\n  // Searchable summary\n  summary             String    @db.Text\n  embedding relatedConversationIds String[]\n  memoryIds           String[]\n\n  // Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  @@index([virtualUserId, startedAt])\n USER PROFILE EVOLUTION\n// ============================================================================\n\n/**\n * UserProfileSnapshot - Evolving user profile snapshots\n * Versioned profile tracking user growth over time\n */\nmodel UserProfileSnapshot {\n  id                  String    @id @default(uuid())\n  virtualUserId       String\n  virtualUser         VirtualUser @relation(fields: [virtualUserId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n\n  avatar              String    @default(\"STRANGER\") // UserAvatar enum\n  version             Int       @default(1)\n\n  // Core identity (JSON for flexibility)\n  identity            Json      @db.JsonB\n  // { displayName, role, company, teamSize, plan, expertise }\n\n  // Preferences\n  preferences         Json      @db.JsonB\n  // { communicationStyle, technicalLevel, responseFormat, timezone, language }\n\n  // Topic expertise map\n  topicExpertise      Json      @db.JsonB\n  // Record<string, { level, questionsAsked, lastInteraction }>\n\n  // Behavioral patterns\n  behavior            Json      @db.JsonB\n  // { avgSessionLength, returnFrequency, escalationRate, ... }\n\n  // Active concerns\n  activeConcerns Timestamps\n  createdAt           DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n  lastEvolvedAt       DateTime  @default(now()) @db.Timestamptz(6)\n\n  @@index([virtualUserId])\n
```
